text: I have traveled a lot, and I have met many interesting people.
Today you tell the story of one of those moments, from start to finish.

While I was cooking, the phone rang.
I was driving home when I noticed a small dog.
We were walking in the market when, suddenly, she smiled.
I was looking at the bowls. Meanwhile, my husband took photos.
You look happy today, Sandra. Did something nice happen?
Yes! This morning, while I was waiting for the bus, something unexpected happened.
Really? Tell me what happened.
I was reading on my phone when, suddenly, I noticed an old friend across the street.
No way! Someone you had not seen for a long time?
Exactly. At that moment, I realized how much I had missed her. We talked for an hour!

While I was waiting, something unexpected happened.
Suddenly, I noticed something strange.
At that moment, I realized what was happening.
Meanwhile, everyone else was still waiting.
It was a completely unexpected end to the day.

Let me tell you about something that happened to me last year. I was driving home from work on a normal Friday evening. The traffic was slow, and I was thinking about dinner. Suddenly, the car in front of me stopped. While I was braking, I noticed a small dog running across the road. I stopped just in time. The dog was scared, so I got out and picked it up. It had no collar. Meanwhile, a woman was running toward me, waving her arms. She was crying. It was her dog. At that moment, I realized how lucky we both were. She thanked me again and again. It was a completely unexpected end to an ordinary day.

I want to tell you about a trip I took a few years ago. My family and I were visiting a small town in the mountains. One afternoon, we were walking through the old market. While we were looking at some handmade bowls, I noticed an old woman sitting in the corner. She was painting. Her work was beautiful. Suddenly, she looked up and smiled at me. She said something in a language I did not understand, but the moment felt warm and kind. Meanwhile, my husband was taking photos of everything. At that moment, I realized that the best part of travel is not the famous places. It is the unexpected people you meet along the way.

First, set the scene: say what was happening. Then introduce a sudden action with suddenly or when. Next, describe what you noticed at that moment. After that, explain what happened next. Finally, say how you felt about the whole experience.

It was a completely unexpected end to the day.
